title: Joby Air Taxi: Dubai's Urban Air Mobility Pioneer
text: Joby Aviation is preparing to launch the world's first commercial air taxi service in Dubai during 2026, marking a major milestone for electric vertical takeoff and landing aircraft technology. A decade ago, Uber's Elevate initiative envisioned electric aircraft ferrying passengers across congested cities. Though Uber eventually abandoned the project, Joby—founded in 2009 and backed by over $3 billion in funding, including $900 million from Toyota—acquired and revived that vision when they purchased the Elevate program in 2020. The company has secured an exclusive six-year operating agreement with Dubai's Roads and Transport Authority. Working with UK-based Skyports, Joby will establish four vertiports strategically located near Dubai International Airport, Dubai Mall, Atlantis the Royal resort, and American University in Dubai. Joby's piloted S4 aircraft features six electric motors, each producing 236 kilowatts of peak output. Initially, operations will involve nonpaying passenger flights and testing before transitioning to full commercial service with paying customers. Dubai is permitting early operations before Joby receives FAA type certification.
